mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of 2024-07-01 suggests potential easing cycle ahead
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals labor market deterioration that increases recession risk and equity risk premium, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice risk.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% as of 2024-07-01 provides baseline for deterioration detection
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite unemployment deterioration signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) over a 2-month window signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, reducing earnings growth expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month simultaneously
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the correlation between joint RSAFS-HOUST weakness and subsequent SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over a 3-month window signals supply-side weakness and capacity utilization stress, reducing profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.9757 (revised) shows recent downward revision from 102.8887
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and rise in unemployment rate (UNRATE) over a 2-month window signals accelerating labor market deterioration that increases recession probability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premium expands.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100K month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 157748.0K (revised) shows recent downward revision from 158723.0K
regime_hint: labor market inflection point in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of co-triggered instances despite joint labor weakness, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) when tested against single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized over a quarter, it signals late-cycle slowdown that reduces equity earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices terminal growth rates.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 1.5% in current quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23286.508 (revised) with high revision risk indicates data uncertainty
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of low-growth quarters, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 3.5% year-over-year, it signals restrictive monetary policy in a high-inflation regime that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% and CPIAUCSL at 313.534 (vintage) indicate restrictive stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of restrictive-inflation regime instances, or the interaction effect between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) over a 3-month window signals strengthening residential demand and forward-looking economic confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1327.0K (revised) provides baseline for growth detection
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with housing recovery
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following HOUST increases are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
